verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B (ATP synthase F1 beta subunit) is canonically located on the inner mitochondrial membrane as the catalytic core of mitochondrial ATP synthase. However, multiple independent studies document its ectopic display on the outer/plasma membrane of tumor cells and endothelial cells ('ecto-ATP synthase'). Flow cytometry and surface-antibody/aptamer studies confirm the beta subunit is accessible on the extracellular face of aggressive breast, prostate, lung, liver, and leukemia cancer cell lines, as well as endothelial cells, but is absent or low on normal non-transformed cells. This is a well-documented cell-state-induced (oncogenic/transformed-state) surface translocation from mitochondria to the plasma membrane outer leaflet.
